text:
There was a little old lady named Martha. She lived in a cozy treehouse. One spring morning, she heard a bird sing a peculiar song. Following the sound, she found a shiny red scarab beetle. Curious, she picked it up. The beetle buzzed her earlobe; she blinked. It scuttled away.

Martha smiled. That night she dreamt of adventures on a treasure hunt. The next day she decided to collect those scarabs. They seemed significant. Each collected one. With an odd collection, she ventured into town. Injured mice whispered to her, thanking her. Martha returned home with bags of tiny treasures. Not a treasure chest, just little insects.

Martha never looked at scarabs the same way again. Years passed. She became a famous insect curator. The scarabs became symbols of peace - every year, she gifted them to veterans.

That's our Martha.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
